mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates suppress risk-free discount rates, which reduces the cost of capital for corporations and increases the present value of future earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean <= 0% or t-test p > 0.05) during months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances where UNRATE falls more than 0.3pp in 3 months are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in real retail sales and industrial production indicates broad-based coordination between consumer demand and manufacturing supply, amplifying earnings growth expectations beyond the independent effects of either series and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO increase month-over-month, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not significantly higher than when only one increases, with a difference in means t-test p-value exceeding 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns when monetary policy is accommodative, because capacity expansion under low interest rates reflects genuine demand growth rather than inflationary overheating.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 1.0%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.5 percent signals accelerating pricing power and nominal revenue growth for corporations, which lifts expected earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered months where CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.5% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals durable labor market tightening coupled with construction sector momentum, and the combination predicts positive equity returns beyond the simple sum of individual series effects over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In a regression of 60-day S&P 500 returns on PAYEMS and HOUST, the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at the 5 percent level or carries a negative sign over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth exceeding 0.5 percent signals above-trend aggregate demand expansion, which leads to upward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters where GDPC1 QoQ growth exceeds 0.5% are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A wide negative spread between the federal funds rate and trailing consumer price inflation proxies an aggressively negative real policy rate, which forces capital out of fixed income and into equities seeking yield, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL momentum shows no significant predictive correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.
